title: ericameria viscidiflora var. latifolia
intro: Ericameria viscidiflora var. latifolia, also known as the broad-leaved sticky rabbitbrush, is a shrubby perennial plant native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful for treating wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate cold and flu symptoms, making it a popular herbal remedy. Herbal preparations of Ericameria viscidiflora var. latifolia include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
